Sayed Rashid is a Java Developer with contact information including email (sayed.rashid@email.com) and links to his GitHub and LinkedIn profiles, as well as a personal website (sayedrashid.com).

Sayed Rashid completed his M.C.A. in January 2018 from Pune University, Maharashtra, India, following a B.C.A. from Amravati University in January 2015, an H.S.C. in January 2012, and an S.S.C. in January 2010, both also from Amravati University.

From 2020 to 2021, Sayed Rashid worked as a Full Stack Java Developer at Salcluster Technologies LLP in Pune, India, where he developed web applications and three projects using Java Servlet, JSP, Ajax, and other web technologies.

From 2019 to 2020, Sayed Rashid was a Software Developer at OmegaSoft Technologies Pvt. Ltd. in Pune, India, developing solutions using Java Spring Hibernate, AngularJs, and other technologies, applying MVC Architecture and Service-based Programming.

In early 2019, Sayed Rashid interned as a Software Developer on a Client Project called Employment Times in Pune, India, developing a website with Java, Spring, Hibernate, AngularJs, Ajax, and MySQL, including modules for Admin Panel, E-paper Posting, Subscriptions, and Payment processing.

Sayed Rashid completed several projects including GST And Sales Billing Softwares (1.5 months), Dinman News Website (1 month), and Agri Management Website (1.5 months), all utilizing Java, JavaScript, JQuery, Ajax, and MySQL.
Sayed Rashid holds certificates in Java Development from Unanth Technical Institute and SoloLearn, as well as a Command Line Crash Course Certificate from Udemy.
Sayed Rashid's skills include programming languages such as Java (Servlet, JSP, Spring Boot).
The listed technologies include HTML5, CSS3, Bootstrap, JavaScript, JQuery, Ajax, and AngularJs.
The database used is MySQL.
The IDEs and tools mentioned are Eclipse, Spring Tool Suite, NetBeans, Sublime Text, and Atom.
The operating systems listed are Windows XP, 7, 8, and 10.

JSP, or JavaServer Pages, is a technology born from the heart of Java, a cornerstone for building dynamic web applications. It’s less a standalone entity and more a powerful extension of Java itself, allowing developers to embed Java code directly within HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. Think of it as the engine that breathes life into static webpages, transforming them into interactive experiences. 

Sayed Rashid, a Full Stack Java Developer at Salcluster Technologies LLP, wielded JSP skillfully between 2020 and 2021, utilizing it alongside Java Servlet, Ajax, and other web technologies to construct fully functional web applications – three distinct projects benefiting from its capabilities. He demonstrates a clear proficiency in its application, showcasing its practical relevance in modern web development. 

JSP isn’t merely a tool *used* in web application creation; it’s fundamentally *part* of the Java ecosystem. It’s a language within a language, a method for Java to directly influence the presentation layer of the web. It allows for the creation of content that changes based on user input, database interactions, or any other dynamic factor, making it a vital component for any web application requiring a personalized or interactive experience.

Sayed Rashid is a driven Java Developer, steadily building a portfolio of web applications and software solutions since completing his M.C.A. from Pune University in 2018. His foundation began with a B.C.A. from Amravati University in 2015, demonstrating a consistent commitment to formal education in computer applications. He’s a practical coder, evidenced by projects undertaken throughout his academic career and professional experience – from GST and Sales Billing Software to the more complex Agri Management and Dinman News Websites, all built with a core skillset encompassing Java, JavaScript, JQuery, Ajax, and MySQL. 

Rashid’s professional journey includes roles at Salcluster Technologies LLP, where he worked as a Full Stack Java Developer, and OmegaSoft Technologies Pvt. Ltd. as a Software Developer. He’s comfortable applying established architectural patterns like MVC and service-based programming, and proficient in frameworks like Spring and Hibernate. An early internship on the ‘Employment Times’ project provided valuable experience developing a full-featured website, including admin panels, subscription services, and payment processing. 

He actively seeks to refine his skills, holding certifications in Java Development from Unanth Technical Institute and SoloLearn, alongside a Udemy course in Command Line proficiency. Rashid maintains an online presence through his personal website (sayedrashid.com), GitHub, and LinkedIn profiles, showcasing his work and connecting with the wider developer community. He’s a dedicated professional with a clear trajectory in full-stack Java development.

Ajax isn’t a person, but a vital tool in the hands of builders like Sayed Rashid. A quietly powerful technology, it’s the unseen current that breathes life into web applications, allowing pages to update seamlessly without disruptive reloads. Rashid wields Ajax alongside Java, JavaScript, and MySQL, shaping dynamic experiences for users. 

He first honed his skills with Ajax during a 2019 internship at Employment Times, crafting modules for admin panels, e-paper delivery, and secure payment processing. This foundation quickly expanded as he moved to Salcluster Technologies, where he integrated Ajax into multiple projects – from robust billing software to the dynamic displays of news and agricultural management websites. 

Ajax isn’t flashy; it’s the reliable engine behind responsive interfaces, the subtle force that makes websites feel fluid and modern. It’s a cornerstone of Rashid’s toolkit, a testament to his ability to build engaging and functional web experiences. It’s a technology he clearly understands, consistently employing it to enhance the user experience across diverse projects.

India is a land of ancient roots and burgeoning modernity, a vast and complex nation where tradition and technology intertwine. It’s a place where the echoes of millennia-old civilizations resonate alongside the hum of a rapidly developing digital landscape. Within its borders lie diverse geographies, from the towering Himalayas to the sun-drenched coasts, and a kaleidoscope of cultures, languages, and beliefs. 

The city of Pune, nestled within the state of Maharashtra, exemplifies this dynamic spirit. It’s a hub of education, evidenced by institutions like Pune University, which in January 2018 conferred a Master of Computer Applications degree upon Sayed Rashid – a testament to India’s growing emphasis on technological advancement. Rashid’s educational journey, beginning with secondary schooling at Amravati University in 2010 and progressing through Bachelor and Master degrees, mirrors the aspirations of a generation seeking opportunity within this evolving nation. 

Pune also serves as a focal point for India’s IT sector. From 2019 to 2021, Rashid honed his skills as a software developer within its thriving tech companies – first at OmegaSoft Technologies, building solutions with Java Spring Hibernate and AngularJs, and later at Salcluster Technologies, crafting web applications with Java Servlet and JSP. His internship on the “Employment Times” project in early 2019, developing a full-featured website with modules for administration, content management, and secure transactions, further illustrates India’s role as a global player in software development and digital innovation. 

India, therefore, isn’t merely a geographical location; it’s an ecosystem fostering talent, driving technological progress, and offering a platform for individuals like Sayed Rashid to contribute to a future shaped by both its rich heritage and its ambitious vision. It’s a nation where education, opportunity, and innovation converge, creating a compelling narrative of growth and transformation.

MySQL is a robust and reliable relational database management system, a quiet workhorse powering the backend of numerous digital creations. It isn’t flashy, but it’s *essential*. A cornerstone technology for developers like Sayed Rashid, it forms the foundation upon which applications are built, storing and organizing the critical data that drives functionality. Rashid first encountered MySQL during a 2019 internship at Employment Times in Pune, India, where it served as the database for a comprehensive website encompassing admin panels, e-paper distribution, subscription management, and secure payment processing. 

Its versatility quickly became apparent as Rashid continued to leverage MySQL across a diverse portfolio of projects. From the intricate logic of GST and Sales Billing Software to the dynamic content of the Dinman News Website and the data-driven needs of an Agri Management Website, MySQL consistently proved its adaptability. It’s a pragmatic choice, favored for its stability and compatibility with popular languages like Java and JavaScript, often paired with frameworks like Spring and Hibernate. While it doesn’t take center stage, MySQL is the silent architect, ensuring data integrity and efficient retrieval – the unseen engine driving the user experience. It simply *works*, allowing developers to focus on building compelling applications rather than wrestling with database complexities.

Pune is a city steeped in academic and technological ambition, a rising hub within the larger landscape of Maharashtra, India. It’s a place where careers are forged and futures built, particularly for those drawn to the digital world. The city’s namesake university serves as a foundational stepping stone, evidenced by the journey of individuals like Sayed Rashid, who began his education there, culminating in an M.C.A. in 2018. 

Pune isn’t merely a place of learning, but a practical proving ground. It’s a magnet for tech companies – Salcluster Technologies LLP and OmegaSoft Technologies Pvt. Ltd. both call it home – offering opportunities for recent graduates to apply their skills. Rashid’s trajectory reflects this, moving from an internship on the ‘Employment Times’ project – a web platform encompassing admin panels, e-paper delivery, and payment systems – to full-fledged roles developing web applications and complex solutions using Java, Spring, Hibernate, and Angular technologies. 

The city breathes innovation, fostering a climate where developers can hone their craft, building projects with modern tools and architectures. It’s a place where theoretical knowledge gained within Pune University’s walls translates directly into tangible, real-world applications, shaping the digital landscape of India and beyond. Pune is, in essence, a crucible for technological talent.

Java is a versatile and foundational technology, a workhorse in the hands of developers like Sayed Rashid. It’s a language of practical application, readily employed to build robust web applications and software solutions. Rashid first encountered Java during a 2019 internship on the “Employment Times” project in Pune, where it formed the core of a full-stack website alongside Spring, Hibernate, and front-end technologies. This wasn’t a fleeting encounter; Java consistently appears in Rashid’s portfolio, powering projects like GST and Sales Billing Software, the Dinman News Website, and an Agri Management Website. 

It’s a language capable of handling diverse tasks, from the intricacies of payment processing and subscription management to the dynamic display of news and agricultural data. Java isn’t monolithic, however. It encompasses specific frameworks and tools – Servlet and JSP for server-side functionality, and the modern, streamlined Spring Boot for building standalone, production-ready applications – allowing developers to tailor its power to specific needs. It’s a reliable, established presence in the software development landscape, and a key skill in Rashid’s toolkit.
